title: SEPPA pm-decision-tools.ts: migrate from local filesystem JSON to PostgreSQL

context.discovery: Phase 1 CLI agent report (2026-03-15) confirmed that pm-decision-tools.ts (apps/mcp-server/src/services/pm-decision-tools.ts, lines 124, 168, 237, 297, 434) reads from local filesystem paths: .roadmap/projects/{projectId}/*.json
existingStores: PostgreSQL (DigitalOcean Managed, port 25060) — used by dashboard, MCP GraphQL resolvers (decision-lifecycle.resolvers.ts), API Gateway
JSON files on /opt/ectropy/ droplet filesystem — used ONLY by pm-decision-tools.ts (SEPPA)
context.frameworkAlignment: MCP server already declares service-postgresql as a dependency in infrastructure-catalog.json. decision-lifecycle.resolvers.ts already uses Prisma. pm-decision-tools.ts is the only outlier.

problemStatement: The local filesystem JSON store breaks under every enterprise scaling condition: multiple tenants (no isolation), multiple servers (files not shared), blue/green deploy (container reset clears files), disaster recovery (not backed up), horizontal scaling (stateless containers cannot share local files). It is also a source of demo instability — if the container restarts, seed data disappears. A solo founder cannot afford a bifurcated data store that requires synchronization, audit, and backup coordination.
decision: pm-decision-tools.ts is refactored to query PostgreSQL (via Prisma, using the existing client already available in the MCP server) for all read operations. JSON file reads are deleted entirely. The function signatures exposed to MCP tool handlers are preserved exactly — only the internal implementation changes. This makes SEPPA's data source consistent with every other reader in the stack.
principles: Single source of truth: PostgreSQL is the canonical runtime data store
Lowest energy state: one store, no synchronization, no drift
Horizontal scalability: stateless MCP containers can read from managed DB
Tenant isolation: project_id FK already provides isolation; tenant_id RLS is a future extension with zero code changes in pm-decision-tools.ts
Crash resilience: managed PostgreSQL has automated backups and failover; droplet filesystem has neither

alternatives.option: Keep JSON files, sync from PostgreSQL on write
alternatives.reason: Two stores require synchronization logic. Synchronization logic has failure modes. Failure modes crash demos. This is the opposite of the lowest energy state.

alternatives.option: Keep JSON files, accept as prototype pattern
alternatives.reason: Erik's explicit principle: no tech debt. The refactor is one service, one file. The cost of doing it now is lower than the cost of carrying it.

alternatives.option: Migrate to PostgreSQL first, then seed
alternatives.reason: Correct order. Seed targets the canonical store. No dual-write complexity. Demo is stable because data lives where managed backups exist.

changes: Remove: fs.readFileSync / fs.existsSync / JSON.parse calls for decisions, voxels, inspections
Add: PrismaClient instantiation (or import shared client if one exists in mcp-server)
Add: startup validation — DATABASE_URL must be set or process exits with clear error
Preserve: all function signatures and return type shapes (no changes to tool handler call sites)
Add: JSDoc on all refactored functions
Add: unit tests covering each query path
Replace: saveDecisions(projectId, collection) → prisma.pmDecision.upsert() for each item in collection.decisions (9 call sites)
Replace: saveConsequences, saveInspections, saveToleranceOverrides, saveVoxels — same upsert pattern per collection
Make all load/save functions async — callers already await
Add bidirectional transform layer: Prisma flat row ↔ JSON Collection shape
PENDING: consequences and tolerance-overrides tables — confirm Prisma schema before migrating those two. Decisions, voxels, inspections migrate now.
notChanged: All MCP tool handler files — they call pm-decision-tools.ts functions which have the same signatures

seeding.oldApproach: Dual-write: INSERT into PostgreSQL AND write JSON files to /opt/ectropy/.roadmap/projects/{id}/
seeding.newApproach: PostgreSQL only — single idempotent TypeScript seed script targeting managed DB
seeding.benefit: Seed data is durable across container restarts, deploys, and DR events

commitConvention:
refactor(mcp): migrate SEPPA pm-decision-tools.ts from filesystem JSON to PostgreSQL

Enterprise Excellence. Schema-First. No Shortcuts.
Deprecation: local .roadmap/projects/{id}/*.json files — no replacement (PostgreSQL is canonical store)
